mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending and corporate profitability, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >70% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns, or the predictive IC drops below -0.01 during confirmed early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 3.5% annual threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where inflation breaches the threshold, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal coincides with negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or positive GDP growth periods yield negative equity returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of industrial production contraction signals an imminent supply-side recession, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The contraction signal fails to predict negative returns, with positive 5-day returns occurring in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during confirmed late-cycle phases.
